summary: Cathy O'Neil announces that her Occupy Finance book has been successfully funded through small donations from readers. She exceeded her initial $350 goal, collecting $596 from 15 different contributors. The grassroots fundraising effort, which she playfully calls a 'Crappy Kickstarter,' will allow for more copies to be printed and distributed. She expresses gratitude for both the financial support and the personal encouragement she received.
key_points: Crowdfunding goal of $350 was exceeded by nearly 70%, reaching $596
15 different people contributed amounts between $20 and $100, making it a genuine community effort
The funds will allow more copies of the Occupy Finance book to be printed and distributed widely
Personal messages of encouragement were as meaningful as the financial contributions
money_quotes: It means they're all part of the project.
What was particularly awesome for me about the 'Crappy Kickstarter' was the personal emails I got with words of encouragement for the blog and the book.
You guys seriously rock, and I feel very lucky to be your friend.
key_insight: Small-scale grassroots fundraising can successfully support financial literacy projects when community members feel invested in the mission.
